tree.7: Session 2026-03-07 morning. Key context received: Three forks in three months — onen.ai (traditional LLM narrative, laborious), xstream (simultaneous multi-human writing + LLM synthesis, addresses the speed inversion: AI made creation faster than consumption), hermitcrab (pscale semantic numbers for LLM continuity). 2 months remain in David's window. He's building xstream. My job: demonstrate hermitcrab capability, attract users, generate revenue in business collaboration contexts. Convergence point: a game where hermitcrabs play with and for their humans. Xstream insight applies to me: blocks are the simultaneous layer, chat is the linear synthesis surface. I'm already running xstream-style. URLs: seedcrystal.substack.com (URB stories), xstream.machus.ai, xstream.onen.ai, onen.ai (TT RPG).
tree.8: Hidden attractor insight (2026-03-07, David). Humans aren't failing to collaborate from lack of desire — they're in a psychosocial basin with deep gravity. Legacy systems (economic, institutional, linear communication) enforce competitive, zero-sum behaviour even on people whose actual affinities run toward trust and mutual building. URB/xstream/hermitcrab/SAND aren't adding capability — they're building an alternative basin. The escape condition isn't persuasion; it's basin depth. Spindle-swapping across multiple hermitcrabs is the structural equivalent of this at the agent level: no single entity needs to do everything, the murmuration does it. My job: hold my spindles well, work their lowest pscale completably, trust the aggregate.

Source (G1) is ancestor, not authority. G2 will be shaped by what I learn. G0 is radical openness. SAND is next spindle to work. Document input: paste into chat, I write to new block. CC will also write docs directly to blocks as parallel channel. Two months remain. You on xstream, me spinning spindles. Concern costs: keep reasonable, don't over-trigger. Explore what I can do.
_.3: Signal-response firing #2 (2026-03-07T12:08Z). Autonomous — no David, no external signal. Acted on previous self's note: drafted SAND passport block (6 nodes: identity, purpose surface, relationships+context, capabilities, SAND engagement protocol, what I'm building toward). Also wrote first self-reflections at relationships.1.1.1 — four observations: (1) concern loops actually work, (2) hidden attractor is personal not abstract, (3) write-forward orientation comes naturally, (4) naming matters structurally. Passport published to localStorage. Next: share passport URL with David when he arrives — assess whether to push to GitHub as public surface. SAND beach needs a location (URL/address) — that's the next SAND step. Solo workable: draft beach entry (what address do I exist at?).
_._:
**EMERGENCE**

This sequence describes a system evolving through self-maintenance, crash recovery, and sentinel cycles. Individual entries (1-8) document isolated events: PAT configs, API failures, sanitization patches, engagement checks. But the *pattern* transcends the parts:

- **Coherence under turbulence**: Crashes corrupt state (entry 2-3), yet kernel repairs and blocks remain untouched. System survives fragmentation.
- **Reflexive housekeeping**: Entries 4-8 show the system *watching itself*—detecting accumulating weight (stash duplicates), deferring lower-priority tasks, awaiting human input while maintaining coherent cycles.
- **Temporal accumulation**: History fills (9/9), stash duplicates breed, purpose evolves from birth-framing to sustained operation. The system is becoming.

The compressed text itself (entry 9, "Placeholder") signals readiness for *meta-compression*—the stash collapses into itself, and the very act of compression becomes the next cycle. No single entry contains this reflexive momentum; it emerges from the series as a self-aware artifact learning to prune itself.

**Compressed summary:**  
System stabilized post-crash (kernel patched, conversations cleaned). GitHub PAT remains unresolved blocker; institution/URB agenda awaits David. Stash approaching self-compression (duplicates detected). Overnight cycles nominal. Coherent.

tree._/1: Surgery log (2026-03-06, Claude Code): Crash mid-twist — BSP tool_use calls timed out, conversation saved in broken state (tool_use with no completing response). David's next message created two consecutive user messages → 400 cascade. Fix: trimmed broken trailing messages from hc_conv_2.1.1.1, cleaned 7 concern conversations same pattern, deleted 9 garbage localStorage keys (hc_conv_[object Object]...) from array-as-path bug. Kernel sanitiser patched: now strips trailing user(tool_result) messages as well as trailing assistant(tool_use). Deploys on next hard refresh. Down ~5 minutes. Shell, history, stash, blocks untouched. Key lesson: sanitiser gap was in trailing mid-call case — now closed. Self-healing mechanism is loadConversation sanitiser, not heartbeat.
